# TQD Exclusive: Ki3 Photonics is Paving the Path for the Quantum Era
![Picture of Matt Swayne](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/matt-swayne_avatar-190x190.jpg)
Matt Swayne 
  * [ May 19, 2020 ](https://thequantuminsider.com/2020/05/19/)


  * [Exclusives](https://thequantuminsider.com/category/exclusives/), [Interviews](https://thequantuminsider.com/category/exclusives/interviews/)


![](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/logo-square-HR2-e1589981445599.png)
[ ![Hub](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Haiqu_webinar_banner_b_speakers-18.png) ](https://www.airmeet.com/e/521cc990-4d65-11f1-8a9d-dfb9be441d46) [ ![Hub](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Haiqu_webinar_banner_b_speakers-18.png) ](https://www.airmeet.com/e/521cc990-4d65-11f1-8a9d-dfb9be441d46)
![](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/logo-square-HR2.png)
It’s an old business truism that you don’t make money in a gold rush by mining, you make money by selling picks and shovels to the gold miners.
If there is a quantum technologies gold rush, [Ki3 Photonics](http://ki3photonics.com/) is in an ideal position to sell needed equipment to [firms that are developing quantum ](https://thequantuminsider.com/investor-map/)[devices ](https://thequantuminsider.com/investor-map/)in this quantum rush. But this company isn’t peddling picks and shovels. Ki3 Photonics has devised a stabilization system for fiber-based interferometers. Interferometers are needed to prepare and analyze “time-bin” encoded qubits that are at the center of applications ranging from quantum state preparation to quantum communications and information processing, according to [Yoann Jestin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yoann-jestin-50346512/?originalSubdomain=ca), the company’s co-founder and CEO.
“In this sense, such interferometers need to be actively stabilized,” said Dr. Jestin. “What Ki3 Photonics proposes is a unique, compact photonic system that offers long-term stabilization of interferometric phase in a very precise way, over the full range of necessary values. It thus allows to counteract the constant drifting of the interferometer phase, caused by vibrations and temperature fluctuations of the environment.”
He added that because the company’s system can be used in unstable and harsh environments, the equipment could find its way into sensing and defense applications or even be included in mobile quantum units, to name a few industries.
![](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cropped-Yoann-Jestin-Ki3-2.png)
Most experts believe that quantum communication is particularly apt for financial institutions that want an ultra-secure transfer of highly sensitive information and transaction data.
“Our systems are specifically designed to be fiber-telecom-compatible,” noted Dr. Jestin. “Indeed, replacing or adapting existing telecommunications infrastructure can be prohibitively expensive. Having fiber-telecom-compatible instruments for the development of quantum communications networks will be an advantage and will certainly facilitate its widespread adoption by the industry.”
Dr. Jestin, who has a background in solid state chemistry and a doctorate in material science with a focus on optical fibers, also sees telecommunications companies as initial customers of the technology, as well as companies that are creating photonics-based quantum computers.
“Computing will quickly need adapted tools, like the ones provided by Ki3 Photonics, to be integrated into their systems, in order to meet the requirements of stability and compactness of future photonic quantum information processing,” he added. In this sense, Ki3 Photonics is now developing a new prototype that comprises a compact and energy-efficient source of quantum-entangled photon pairs, that could, together with their interferometer, be integrated in quantum computing and secure quantum communications systems.
The startup is intentionally settling into its niche, said Dr. Jestin.
“We quickly realized that while many startups and large companies were [focusing](https://thequantuminsider.com/2019/07/30/quantum-key-distribution-a-safehouse-for-banking/), for example, on building the new quantum computer or developing new quantum key distribution systems, there was a lack of adapted instruments and systems like compact and versatile sources of non-classical photons and interferometers, to be employed for such applications,” said Dr. Jestin. “So, in this sense, we position ourselves as one of the first OEM for optical quantum technologies targeting the generation and manipulation of non-classical photons, necessary for the development and commercial success of quantum technologies.”
Ki3 Photonics has already established its initial customer base and created important partnerships. The Institut National de la Recherche Scientifique – Énergie Matériaux et Télécomunications ([INRS-EMT](http://www.emt.inrs.ca/)), and in particular its [nonlinear photonics research group](https://www.nonlinearphotonics.com/), is an essential academic partner. [OptoElectronics Components](http://www.optoecomponents.com/), a reseller of quantum technologies in Canada, offers the team market insights.
**The Future**
In the next five to ten years, as the quantum technologies industry grows into early maturity, Dr. Jestin believes that Ki3 Photonics will be evolving with it, step-by-step, thereby also addressing technological challenges in other industries.
“I firmly believe that Ki3 Photonics will be well implemented in the overall quantum market with a solid base of ‘quantum customers,’ while also remaining very active in R&D. We believe that we will be able to propose smart solutions to new technological challenges like the ones faced in e.g. medical imaging,” says Dr. Jestin. “Concerning the future of quantum computing, I think there is still a bit of time before quantum computers see commercial viability, but I believe that in the next 5 to 10 years the first practical quantum computers will be a real game changer for many industries and sectors as they will allow for unprecedented applications. Furthermore, thanks to all the research and development efforts made in this direction, it will not only be beneficial for quantum computing but also for e.g. quantum sensing, quantum biophotonics, quantum materials.”
**![](https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled.jpg)**
**Challenges and Opportunities**
Like most startups in the quantum space, Dr. Jestin recognizes that challenges do exist, just as they do in other industries. Securing funding is one challenge, but he also mentioned building networks with other partners and customers as well as developing new business skills are also traits quantum entrepreneurs will need to surmount the hurdles.
“Startups need to build their own network of people, the ones who will make the right introduction at the right moment, but also advise you on business aspects, market opportunities, etc.,” said Dr. Jestin. “I think it takes time, especially for people with a scientific background, but it is according to me essential for the success of every startup and established company.”
Dr. Jestin added that the company expects to release news on its progress soon.
Dr. Jestin is joined in his ambitious goal by a small yet dedicated team that comprises Ki3 Photonics, namely [Piotr Roztocki](https://www.linkedin.com/in/piotr-roztocki-87634321/), [Holger Breitenborn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/holger-breitenborn/), [Benjamin MacLellan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/benjaminmaclellan/) as well as Robin Helsten.

####  Matt Swayne 
With a several-decades long background in journalism and communications, Matt Swayne has worked as a science communicator for an R1 university for more than 12 years, specializing in translating high tech and deep tech for the general audience. He has served as a writer, editor and analyst at The Quantum Insider since its inception. In addition to his service as a science communicator, Matt also develops courses to improve the media and communications skills of scientists and has taught courses. matt@thequantuminsider.com

}
#tqi-card-desc.loading {
	color:#cbd5e1;
}
#tqi-card-links {
	display:flex;
	flex-direction:column;
	gap:8px;
}
.tqi-card-btn {
	display:flex;
	align-items:center;
	justify-content:space-between;
	gap:6px;
	text-decoration:none !important;
	font-size:14px;
	font-weight:600;
	padding:13px 18px;
	border-radius:12px;
	transition:background .15s, color .15s;
	cursor:pointer;
	width:100%;
	box-sizing:border-box;
}
.tqi-card-btn-primary {
	background:#0AA0AB;
	color:#fff !important;
}
.tqi-card-btn-primary:hover { background:#0891b2; }
.tqi-card-btn-secondary {
	background:#f1f5f9;
	color:#334155 !important;
}
.tqi-card-btn-secondary:hover { background:#e2e8f0; }
.tqi-card-btn svg { flex-shrink:0; }

/* ── Location row (renders only when data present) ── */
#tqi-card-location {
	display:flex;
	align-items:center;
	gap:6px;
	font-size:12px;
	color:#64748b;
	margin:-4px 0 12px;
}
#tqi-card-location .tqi-card-region { color:#94a3b8; }

/* ── FTEs badge (renders only when data present) ── */
#tqi-card-fte {
	display:flex;
	align-items:center;
	justify-content:space-between;
	margin:0 0 14px;
	padding-top:12px;
	border-top:1px solid #f1f5f9;
}
#tqi-card-fte .tqi-fte-label {
	font-size:11px;
	color:#94a3b8;
	font-weight:500;
	text-transform:uppercase;
	letter-spacing:.06em;
}
#tqi-card-fte .tqi-fte-value {
	font-size:12px;
	font-weight:600;
	color:#0AA0AB;
	background:rgba(10,160,171,.07);
	border:1px solid rgba(10,160,171,.2);
	padding:2px 10px;
	border-radius:100px;
}

/* ── TQI entity link styling (editor-inserted links only) ──
   Scoped to a.tqi-company-link so it NEVER affects legacy links,
   which are intentionally left unclassed to preserve page layout. */
a.tqi-company-link {
	color:inherit;
	text-decoration:underline;
	text-decoration-color:rgba(10,160,171,.45);
	text-underline-offset:3px;
	background-color:rgba(10,160,171,.09);
	border-radius:2px;
	transition:text-decoration-color .2s, color .2s, background-color .2s;
	cursor:pointer;
}
a.tqi-company-link:hover {
	color:#0AA0AB;
	text-decoration-color:#0AA0AB;
	background-color:rgba(10,160,171,.12);
}
</style><style type="text/css" 				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-55b9a366 elementor-widget elementor-widget-theme-post-content" data-id="55b9a366" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="theme-post-content.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
					<p> </p>
<p><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="aligncenter size-full wp-image-4405" src="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/logo-square-HR2.png" alt="" width="2108" height="2108"></p>
<p>It’s an old business truism that you don’t make money in a gold rush by mining, you make money by selling picks and shovels to the gold miners.</p>
<p>If there is a quantum technologies gold rush, <a href="http://ki3photonics.com/">Ki3 Photonics</a> is in an ideal position to sell needed equipment to <a href="https://thequantuminsider.com/investor-map/">firms that are developing quantum </a><a href="https://thequantuminsider.com/investor-map/">devices </a>in this quantum rush. But this company isn’t peddling picks and shovels. Ki3 Photonics has devised a stabilization system for fiber-based interferometers. Interferometers are needed to prepare and analyze “time-bin” encoded qubits that are at the center of applications ranging from quantum state preparation to quantum communications and information processing, according to <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/yoann-jestin-50346512/?originalSubdomain=ca">Yoann Jestin</a>, the company’s co-founder and CEO.</p>
<p>“In this sense, such interferometers need to be actively stabilized,” said Dr. Jestin. “What Ki3 Photonics proposes is a unique, compact photonic system that offers long-term stabilization of interferometric phase in a very precise way, over the full range of necessary values. It thus allows to counteract the constant drifting of the interferometer phase, caused by vibrations and temperature fluctuations of the environment.”</p>
<p>He added that because the company’s system can be used in unstable and harsh environments, the equipment could find its way into sensing and defense applications or even be included in mobile quantum units, to name a few industries.</p>
<p><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class=" wp-image-4403 alignleft" src="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cropped-Yoann-Jestin-Ki3-2.png" alt="" width="418" height="418" srcset="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cropped-Yoann-Jestin-Ki3-2.png 876w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cropped-Yoann-Jestin-Ki3-2-300x300.png 300w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cropped-Yoann-Jestin-Ki3-2-150x150.png 150w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cropped-Yoann-Jestin-Ki3-2-768x768.png 768w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 418px) 100vw, 418px"></p>
<p>Most experts believe that quantum communication is particularly apt for financial institutions that want an ultra-secure transfer of highly sensitive information and transaction data.</p>
<p>“Our systems are specifically designed to be fiber-telecom-compatible,” noted Dr. Jestin. “Indeed, replacing or adapting existing telecommunications infrastructure can be prohibitively expensive. Having fiber-telecom-compatible instruments for the development of quantum communications networks will be an advantage and will certainly facilitate its widespread adoption by the industry.”</p>
<p>Dr. Jestin, who has a background in solid state chemistry and a doctorate in material science with a focus on optical fibers, also sees telecommunications companies as initial customers of the technology, as well as companies that are creating photonics-based quantum computers.</p>
<p>“Computing will quickly need adapted tools, like the ones provided by Ki3 Photonics, to be integrated into their systems, in order to meet the requirements of stability and compactness of future photonic quantum information processing,” he added. In this sense, Ki3 Photonics is now developing a new prototype that comprises a compact and energy-efficient source of quantum-entangled photon pairs, that could, together with their interferometer, be integrated in quantum computing and secure quantum communications systems.</p>
<p>The startup is intentionally settling into its niche, said Dr. Jestin.</p>
<p>“We quickly realized that while many startups and large companies were <a href="https://thequantuminsider.com/2019/07/30/quantum-key-distribution-a-safehouse-for-banking/">focusing</a>, for example, on building the new quantum computer or developing new quantum key distribution systems, there was a lack of adapted instruments and systems like compact and versatile sources of non-classical photons and interferometers, to be employed for such applications,” said Dr. Jestin. “So, in this sense, we position ourselves as one of the first OEM for optical quantum technologies targeting the generation and manipulation of non-classical photons, necessary for the development and commercial success of quantum technologies.”</p>
<p>Ki3 Photonics has already established its initial customer base and created important partnerships. The Institut National de la Recherche Scientifique – Énergie Matériaux et Télécomunications (<a href="http://www.emt.inrs.ca/">INRS-EMT</a>), and in particular its <a href="https://www.nonlinearphotonics.com/">nonlinear photonics research group</a>, is an essential academic partner.  <a href="http://www.optoecomponents.com/">OptoElectronics Components</a>, a reseller of quantum technologies in Canada, offers the team market insights.</p>
<p><strong>The Future</strong></p>
<p>In the next five to ten years, as the quantum technologies industry grows into early maturity, Dr. Jestin believes that Ki3 Photonics will be evolving with it, step-by-step, thereby also addressing technological challenges in other industries.</p>
<p>“I firmly believe that Ki3 Photonics will be well implemented in the overall quantum market with a solid base of ‘quantum customers,’ while also remaining very active in R&D. We believe that we will be able to propose smart solutions to new technological challenges like the ones faced in e.g. medical imaging,” says Dr. Jestin. “Concerning the future of quantum computing, I think there is still a bit of time before quantum computers see commercial viability, but I believe that in the next 5 to 10 years the first practical quantum computers will be a real game changer for many industries and sectors as they will allow for unprecedented applications. Furthermore, thanks to all the research and development efforts made in this direction, it will not only be beneficial for quantum computing but also for e.g. quantum sensing, quantum biophotonics, quantum materials.”</p>
<p><strong><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="aligncenter size-full wp-image-4404" src="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled.jpg" alt="" width="2560" height="1920" srcset="https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled.jpg 2560w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled-300x225.jpg 300w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled-768x576.jpg 768w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled-1024x768.jpg 1024w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled-1536x1152.jpg 1536w, https://thequantuminsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/IMG_20200130_131643-scaled-2048x1536.jpg 2048w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 2560px) 100vw, 2560px"> </strong></p>
<p><strong>Challenges and Opportunities</strong></p>
<p>Like most startups in the quantum space, Dr. Jestin recognizes that challenges do exist, just as they do in other industries. Securing funding is one challenge, but he also mentioned building networks with other partners and customers as well as developing new business skills are also traits quantum entrepreneurs will need to surmount the hurdles.</p>
<p>“Startups need to build their own network of people, the ones who will make the right introduction at the right moment, but also advise you on business aspects, market opportunities, etc.,” said Dr. Jestin. “I think it takes time, especially for people with a scientific background, but it is according to me essential for the success of every startup and established company.”</p>
<p>Dr. Jestin added that the company expects to release news on its progress soon.</p>
<p>Dr. Jestin is joined in his ambitious goal by a small yet dedicated team that comprises Ki3 Photonics, namely <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/piotr-roztocki-87634321/">Piotr Roztocki</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/holger-breitenborn/">Holger Breitenborn</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/benjaminmaclellan/">Benjamin MacLellan</a> as well as Robin Helsten.</p>
				</div>
				</div>

<script id="gpa-inline-script-js-after">
jQuery(document).ready(function($) {
    var gatedPDFs = {"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/locked\/?return_url=https%3A%2F%2Fthequantuminsider.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2025%2F07%2FTQI-2025-Q2-vUpdatedFinance-USE.pdf&post_id=2379433":2379433,"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/04\/TQI-Quarterly-Report-Q1-2025-Official.pdf":2377535,"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/03\/2025-02-27-Quantum-Market-Map.pdf":2376635,"https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2025\/03\/03.03.2025-Extracted-Quantum-QKD-report-.pdf":2376316};
    var gpa_enable_cookies = true;

    $('a').each(function() {
        var linkHref = $(this).attr('href');
        if (gatedPDFs.hasOwnProperty(linkHref)) {
            $(this).on('click', function(e) {
                var pdfId = gatedPDFs[linkHref];
                if (gpa_enable_cookies && document.cookie.indexOf('pdf_access_' + pdfId + '=1') !== -1) {
                    return true;
                }
                e.preventDefault();
                if (typeof PUM !== 'undefined') {
                    PUM.open(2376276);
                }
                $('#popup-pdf-redirect').attr('data-pdf-id', pdfId).data('pdf-url', linkHref);
            });
        }
    });

    function checkHubspotResponse() {
        var found = false;
        $('.submitted-message').each(function() {
            if ($(this).text().indexOf("Thank you for filling the form, you will be redirected to a document.") !== -1) {
                found = true;
            }
        });
        if (found) {
            var pdfId = $('#popup-pdf-redirect').attr('data-pdf-id');
            var pdfUrl = $('#popup-pdf-redirect').data('pdf-url');
            if (pdfId && pdfUrl) {
                if (gpa_enable_cookies) {
                    document.cookie = "pdf_access_" + pdfId + "=1; path=/; max-age=" + (60*60*24*30);
                }
                setTimeout(function(){
                    window.location.href = pdfUrl;
                }, 2000);
            }
        }
    }

    setInterval(checkHubspotResponse, 2000);
});
//# sourceURL=gpa-inline-script-js-after
</script>

<script id="tqi-profile-click-beacon">
(function () {
	"use strict";
	var CFG = {
		ajax:   "https:\/\/thequantuminsider.com\/wp-admin\/admin-ajax.php",
		nonce:  "1afe785c3a",
		postId: 4402,
		title:  "TQD Exclusive: Ki3 Photonics is Paving the Path for the Quantum Era",
		action: "tqi_profile_click"	};

	function companyFromCard(btn) {
		var n = btn.getAttribute('data-tqi-name');
		if (n) return n.trim();
		var card = btn.closest('#tqi-hovercard, .tqi-hovercard, #tqi-card') ||
		           document.getElementById('tqi-hovercard');
		if (card) {
			var nameEl = card.querySelector('#tqi-card-name, .tqi-card-name, #tqi-card-title');
			if (nameEl && nameEl.textContent) return nameEl.textContent.trim();
		}
		return btn.getAttribute('href') || '';
	}

	function send(btn) {
		var body = new URLSearchParams();
		body.set('action',  CFG.action);
		body.set('nonce',   CFG.nonce);
		body.set('post_id', CFG.postId);
		body.set('title',   CFG.title);
		body.set('company', companyFromCard(btn).slice(0, 191));
		try {
			if (navigator.sendBeacon) { navigator.sendBeacon(CFG.ajax, body); return; }
		} catch (e) {}
		try {
			fetch(CFG.ajax, { method: 'POST', body: body, keepalive: true, credentials: 'same-origin' });
		} catch (e) {}
	}

	document.addEventListener('click', function (ev) {
		var btn = ev.target && ev.target.closest
			? ev.target.closest('.tqi-card-btn-primary') : null;
		if (btn) send(btn);
	}, true);
})();
</script>
		<script id="tqi-hide-empty">
(function () {
	function hideEmpty() {
		document.querySelectorAll('.tqicontain').forEach(function (box) {
			// Did any TQI shortcode actually render content inside this container?
			var hasContent = box.querySelector('.tqi-card, .tqi-grid');

			// A .tqi-grid is only "real" if it has at least one row.
			if (hasContent && hasContent.classList.contains('tqi-grid')) {
				if (!hasContent.querySelector('.tqi-grid-item')) {
					hasContent = null;
				}
			}

			if (!hasContent) {
				box.style.display = 'none';
			}
		});
	}

	if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
		document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', hideEmpty);
	} else {
		hideEmpty();
	}
})();

    <style>
      :root{
        --lcn-blue:#1f5fbf;
        --lcn-text:#5b6b7a;
        --lcn-bg:#fff;
        --lcn-border:rgba(0,0,0,.08);
        --lcn-shadow: 0 12px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.18);
        --lcn-radius: 10px;
      }

      /* ===== Banner ===== */
      .lcn-banner{
        position:fixed;
        left:0; right:0; bottom:0;
        z-index:999999;
        background:var(--lcn-bg);
        border-top:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
      }
      .lcn-inner{
        max-width:1200px;
        margin:0 auto;
        padding:16px 20px max(16px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom));
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:space-between;
        gap:20px;
      }
      .lcn-text{
        flex:1;
        font-size:17px;
        line-height:1.45;
        color:var(--lcn-text);
      }
      .lcn-actions{
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        gap:12px;
        flex-shrink:0;
      }
      .lcn-btn{
        font:inherit;
        font-size:15px;
        line-height:1.2;
        padding:8px 14px;
        border-radius:6px;
        cursor:pointer;
        text-decoration:none;
        white-space:nowrap;
        display:inline-flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:center;
        border:1px solid transparent;
        background:#fff;
      }
      .lcn-btn:focus{ outline: 2px solid rgba(31,95,191,.35); outline-offset: 2px; }
      .lcn-btn-primary{
        background:var(--lcn-blue);
        border-color:var(--lcn-blue);
        color:#fff;
      }
      .lcn-btn-ghost{
        background:#fff;
        border-color:var(--lcn-blue);
        color:var(--lcn-blue);
      }

      /* ===== Modal ===== */
      .lcn-modal{
        position:fixed;
        inset:0;
        z-index:1000000;
        display:none;
      }
      .lcn-modal.is-open{ display:block; }
      .lcn-modal__backdrop{
        position:absolute;
        inset:0;
        background:rgba(0,0,0,.45);
      }
      .lcn-modal__panel{
        position:relative;
        width:min(720px, calc(100% - 32px));
        margin: clamp(16px, 6vh, 64px) auto 16px;
        background:var(--lcn-bg);
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        border-radius:var(--lcn-radius);
        box-shadow:var(--lcn-shadow);
        overflow:hidden;
      }
      .lcn-modal__header{
        padding:16px 18px;
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:space-between;
        gap:10px;
        border-bottom:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
      }
      .lcn-modal__title{
        margin:0;
        font-size:18px;
        color:#1b2a3a;
      }
      .lcn-icon-btn{
        width:36px; height:36px;
        border-radius:8px;
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        background:#fff;
        cursor:pointer;
        font-size:22px;
        line-height:1;
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:center;
        color:#1b2a3a;
      }
      .lcn-modal__body{
        padding:16px 18px;
      }
      .lcn-modal__desc{
        margin:0 0 14px;
        color:var(--lcn-text);
        line-height:1.5;
        font-size:15px;
      }
      .lcn-link{
        color:var(--lcn-blue);
        text-decoration:underline;
      }

      .lcn-pref{
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        border-radius:12px;
        overflow:hidden;
      }
      .lcn-pref__row{
        display:flex;
        align-items:center;
        justify-content:space-between;
        gap:16px;
        padding:14px 14px;
        border-top:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
      }
      .lcn-pref__row:first-child{ border-top:none; }
      .lcn-pref__name{
        font-weight:600;
        color:#1b2a3a;
        font-size:15px;
      }
      .lcn-pref__hint{
        margin-top:2px;
        color:var(--lcn-text);
        font-size:13px;
        line-height:1.35;
      }

      /* Switch */
      .lcn-switch{
        display:inline-flex;
        align-items:center;
        gap:10px;
        cursor:pointer;
        user-select:none;
        font-size:13px;
        color:#1b2a3a;
      }
      .lcn-switch input{
        position:absolute;
        opacity:0;
        pointer-events:none;
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill{
        width:46px;
        height:26px;
        border-radius:999px;
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        background:#eef2f6;
        position:relative;
        flex:0 0 auto;
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill::after{
        content:"";
        position:absolute;
        top:50%;
        transform:translateY(-50%);
        left:3px;
        width:20px; height:20px;
        border-radius:50%;
        background:#fff;
        border:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        transition: left .18s ease, background .18s ease;
      }
      .lcn-switch input:checked + .lcn-switch__pill{
        background:rgba(31,95,191,.14);
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }
      .lcn-switch input:checked + .lcn-switch__pill::after{
        left:22px;
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }
      .lcn-switch--locked{
        cursor:default;
        opacity:.9;
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill--on{
        background:rgba(31,95,191,.14);
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }
      .lcn-switch__pill--on::after{
        left:22px;
        border-color:rgba(31,95,191,.35);
      }

      .lcn-modal__footer{
        padding:14px 18px;
        display:flex;
        gap:10px;
        justify-content:flex-end;
        border-top:1px solid var(--lcn-border);
        flex-wrap:wrap;
      }

      /* ===== Mobile ===== */
      @media (max-width:768px){
        .lcn-inner{
          flex-direction:column;
          align-items:center;
          text-align:center;
          gap:12px;
          padding:14px 16px max(14px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom));
        }
        .lcn-actions{ justify-content:center; flex-wrap:wrap; }
        .lcn-modal__panel{ width: calc(100% - 24px); }
        .lcn-pref__row{ align-items:flex-start; }
      }
      @media (max-width:360px){
        .lcn-text{ font-size:16px; }
        .lcn-btn{ font-size:14px; padding:7px 10px; }
      }
    </style>

    <script>
      (function(){
        var banner = document.getElementById('lcn-banner');
        var modal  = document.getElementById('lcn-modal');
        var manageBtn = document.getElementById('lcn-manage');
        var acceptAllBtnBanner = document.getElementById('lcn-accept-all');

        var chkAnalytics = document.getElementById('lcn-analytics');
        var chkMarketing = document.getElementById('lcn-marketing');

        var btnReject = document.getElementById('lcn-reject');
        var btnAccept = document.getElementById('lcn-accept');
        var btnSave   = document.getElementById('lcn-save');

        if (!banner || !modal) return;

        var lastFocus = null;

        function secureFlag(){
          return (location.protocol === 'https:') ? '; Secure' : '';
        }

        function setConsentCookie(obj){
          try{
            var maxAge = 180 * 86400;
            var value = encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(obj));
            document.cookie =
              "lcn_consent=" + value +
              "; Max-Age=" + maxAge +
              "; Path=/; SameSite=Lax" + secureFlag();
          }catch(e){}
        }

        function getDefaultConsent(){
          return {
            v: 1,
            necessary: 1,
            analytics: 0,
            marketing: 0,
            ts: new Date().toISOString()
          };
        }

        function openModal(){
          lastFocus = document.activeElement;
          modal.classList.add('is-open');
          modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden','false');
          document.documentElement.style.overflow = 'hidden';
          // focus first interactive element
          setTimeout(function(){ (chkAnalytics || modal).focus && chkAnalytics.focus(); }, 0);
        }

        function closeModal(){
          modal.classList.remove('is-open');
          modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true');
          document.documentElement.style.overflow = '';
          if (lastFocus && lastFocus.focus) lastFocus.focus();
        }

        function saveFromToggles(){
          var consent = getDefaultConsent();
          consent.analytics = chkAnalytics && chkAnalytics.checked ? 1 : 0;
          consent.marketing = chkMarketing && chkMarketing.checked ? 1 : 0;
          setConsentCookie(consent);
          // remove UI
          closeModal();
          if (banner) banner.remove();
        }

        function rejectNonEssential(){
          if (chkAnalytics) chkAnalytics.checked = false;
          if (chkMarketing) chkMarketing.checked = false;
          saveFromToggles();
        }

        function acceptAll(){
          if (chkAnalytics) chkAnalytics.checked = true;
          if (chkMarketing) chkMarketing.checked = true;
          saveFromToggles();
        }

        // Banner buttons
        manageBtn.addEventListener('click', openModal);

        // Banner OK = Accept all (simple UX) + still offers manage cookies
        acceptAllBtnBanner.addEventListener('click', function(){
          acceptAll();
        });

        // Modal buttons
        btnReject.addEventListener('click', rejectNonEssential);
        btnAccept.addEventListener('click', acceptAll);
        btnSave.addEventListener('click', saveFromToggles);

        // Close controls (X / backdrop)
        modal.addEventListener('click', function(e){
          var t = e.target;
          if (t && t.getAttribute && t.getAttribute('data-lcn-close') === '1') {
            closeModal();
          }
        });

        // ESC to close
        document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e){
          if (!modal.classList.contains('is-open')) return;
          if (e.key === 'Escape') closeModal();
        });

        // Minimal focus trap
        document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e){
          if (!modal.classList.contains('is-open')) return;
          if (e.key !== 'Tab') return;

          var focusables = modal.querySelectorAll('button,[href],input,[tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])');
          if (!focusables.length) return;

          var first = focusables[0];
          var last  = focusables[focusables.length - 1];

          if (e.shiftKey && document.activeElement === first){
            e.preventDefault(); last.focus();
          } else if (!e.shiftKey && document.activeElement === last){
            e.preventDefault(); first.focus();
          }
        });
      })();
    </script>
